task_title: Structural reduction to modular matroids and simplification issues
notes: The original problem is reduced without loss to the case of a loopless simple modular matroid, hence a direct sum of U_{1,1} components and finite projective geometries, equipped with a component-permuting projective duality on its flat lattice. The remaining question is whether that prescribed flat-lattice duality extends to an order-reversing involution of the full Dr poset; no componentwise factorization of Dr should be assumed.

The point-hyperplane polarity alpha and its incidence symmetry are established under the stated modular assumptions, and this gives a complete rank-at-most-3 involution on Dr(M). Extending to all ranks requires proving that the incidence polar of every tropical k-plane is a tropical linear space of complementary rank and satisfies biduality.

The coordinate polar is now proved to be a well-defined involutive rank-reversing bijection on the underlying set of valuated quotients of M, and it extends the flat polarity. What remains to solve the original poset question is the flag-duality lemma: if p quotient q quotient M, then q^perp quotient p^perp. I see no counterexample; the lemma is known in the Boolean case and follows in the q=M boundary case from the quotient proof above.

task_title: Local dual Plücker lemma and rank-four stress test
notes: The requested local Plucker lemma is proved, including rank-four stress tests and degeneracies. In rank 4 this settles the middle-rank Plucker part of the proposed polarity. The remaining gap for the full Dressian involution is global: one still needs to prove the polar object satisfies all quotient-incidence conditions and that incidences with rank-one and rank-three tropical subspaces are reversed, giving a genuine order-reversing poset involution.

The point-annihilator route avoids the missing full mixed-rank flag-duality lemma. The key observation is apartmentwise: p^dagger restricted to the polar basis is the ordinary dual of p restricted to the original basis, and ordinary tropical annihilator duality supplies exactly the needed incidence conditions.

task_title: Direct proof of the mixed-rank flag-duality lemma
notes: The mixed-rank lemma is proved. Together with t3's construction of the dagger operation as an involution on valuated quotients, this gives the desired order reversal on the relative Dressian under the stated modular polarity hypotheses.

task_title: Skeptical referee check of the claimed full proof
notes: No fatal counterexample was found. The proof is acceptable in strategy but not yet in final form: it must explicitly prove simplification invariance, dependent/boundary cases for p^dagger, the interval codimension-one duality lemma, well-definedness via the point-annihilator theorem, and flat-complement compatibility.

The core theorem in the simple modular case is proved: the dagger construction is well-defined, gives a valuated quotient of M, is involutive, extends the flat-lattice polarity, and reverses quotient order. No additional gap remains in the stated simple modular setting.

task_title: Rigorous non-simple reduction and simplification invariance
notes: The arbitrary finite matroid case is reduced rigorously to the loopless simple modular simplification. What remains for the full theorem is only the construction of the order-reversing involution on Dr(S) in the simple modular case.

task_title: Adversarial referee check of the final theorem proof strategy
notes: Verdict: sound after repairs, not sound as written. The main remaining work is to state the missing lemmas and conventions precisely, especially annihilator duality with infinities, local repeated/dependent cases, and simplification compatibility.

task_title: Referee-proof that the dagger construction gives a valuated quotient
notes: The construction p -> p^dagger is now fully proved in the finite simple modular case independently of any global order-reversal argument. No gap remains for flat-constancy, Plucker relations, quotient incidence with M, involutivity, or flat-quotient compatibility in this setting.

task_title: Referee-proof the point-annihilator theorem and order reversal
notes: The point-annihilator theorem is fully proved in the finite simple loopless modular case, with affine conventions, Boolean-apartment details, infinity-coordinate handling, projectivization, and order reversal. The proof assumes the previously established fact that the flat-coordinate polar p^dagger is a valuated quotient and that dagger is involutive.

task_title: Self-contained simplification invariance theorem for relative Dressians
notes: The simplification reduction is fully proved: Dr(M) is canonically identified with Dr(si(M)) by zero-parallel extension, the embedded flat subposets match, and any flat-lattice anti-involution plus a Dressian involution for the simplification transfers back to M.

task_title: Adversarial referee check of the complete proof
notes: No counterexample was found. The proof should be accepted after explicitly citing the standard valuated-matroid representation and quotient theorems, adding the finite-q-basis independence patch in the simplification reduction, and standardizing affine/projective conventions for the all-infinity and rank-zero cases.

task_title: Final adversarial editorial audit and standard-theorem dependency check
notes: No fatal obstruction was found. The final proof should explicitly state the standard valuated-matroid theorems, use affine cones throughout the technical arguments, replace the simplification-descent proof with the coordinate-cocircuit version, and add the rank-zero/full-rank projective boundary paragraphs.

task_title: Final dependency audit and lemma-chain certification
notes: No remaining gap found. The final write-up should explicitly insert the lemma that a finite geometric lattice with an order-reversing involution is modular, state the standard valuated-matroid facts listed above, work affinely throughout, and projectivize only at the end.

task_title: Final adversarial referee pass on the complete proof strategy
notes: No fatal flaw found. The final write-up should include the listed standard dependencies, affine conventions, the modularity patch, the coordinate-cocircuit simplification proof, explicit boundary cases, and the global-cocircuit proof of the annihilator converse.
